volumeTitle: The Little Carthaginian. Pseudolus. The Rope
label: The Little Carthaginian (Section 5.2.1066 - Section 5.3.1132)

sections.label: Section 5.2.1066
sections.original: Patrem atque matrem viverent vellem tibi .
sections.translation: I could wish that your father and mother were alive .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1067
sections.translation: Are they dead ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1067b
sections.translation: So it is , a thing which I bore with much grief ; for your mother Ampsigura was my cousin-german ; your father — he was my uncle ’ s son ,

sections.label: Section 5.2.1070
sections.original: et is me heredem fecit , quom suom obiit diem ,
sections.translation: and when he died he made me his heir ; of whom being deprived by death , I am greatly affected . But if it is the fact that you really are the son of Sachon , there ought to be a mark upon your left hand , a bite which an ape gave you when a child , playing with it .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1075
sections.translation: Show it , that I may look at it ;

sections.label: Section 5.2.1075b
sections.translation: open your hand .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1075c
sections.translation: Look , if you like ; see , there it is .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1076
sections.translation: My kinsman , welcome to you !

sections.label: Section 5.2.1076b
sections.translation: And welcome to you , Agorastocles ! I seem to myself to be born again , in having found you .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1078
sections.original: Pol istám rem vobis bene evenisse gaudeo .
sections.translation: By my troth , I ’ m delighted that this matter has fallen out so happily for you . ( To HANNO .) And would you decline to take advice ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1079b
sections.translation: Really , I should wish to be advised .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1080
sections.translation: His father ’ s property ought to be restored to the son ; it ’ s fair that he should have the property which his father possessed .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1082
sections.translation: I wish no otherwise ; everything shall be restored . I ’ ll give his own property to him all safe , when he comes there .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1084
sections.original: Facito sis reddas , etsi hic habitabit , tamen .
sections.translation: Take care and restore it , will you , even though he should live here still .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1085
sections.original: Quin mea quoque iste habebit , si quid me fuat .
sections.translation: Nay but , he shall have my own as well , if anything should happen to me [*] .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1086
sections.original: Festivom facinus venit mi in mentem modo .
sections.translation: A pleasant project has just now come into my mind ,

sections.label: Section 5.2.1087
sections.translation: What ’ s that ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1087b
sections.translation: There ’ s need of your assistance .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1087c
sections.translation: Tell me what you wish . Really , you shall have my services just as you please . What is the business ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1089b
sections.translation: Can you act the cheat ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1090
sections.translation: Towards an enemy , I can ; to a friend , it would be mere folly .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1091
sections.translation: I ’ faith , it is an enemy of his .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1091b
sections.translation: I could do him a mischief with pleasure .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1092
sections.translation: He ’ s in love with a person who belongs to a Procurer .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1092b
sections.translation: I deem that he acts discreetly .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1093
sections.translation: This Procurer lives close at hand .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1093b
sections.translation: I could do him a mischief with pleasure .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1094
sections.original: Ei duae puellae sunt meretrices servolae
sections.translation: He has two slave girls , courtesans ,

sections.label: Section 5.2.1095
sections.original: sorores : earum hic alteram efflictim perit ,
sections.translation: sisters ; one of these he is desperately in love with , nor has he ever taker any liberties with her .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1096b
sections.translation: It ’ s an unhappy kind of passion .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1097
sections.translation: The Procurer plays upon him .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1097b
sections.translation: He ’ s enhancing his own profits thereby .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1098
sections.original: Hic illi malam rem dare volt .
sections.translation: He wishes to do him an evil turn .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1098b
sections.translation: He ’ s right , if he does do it .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1099
sections.original: Nunc hoc consilium capio et hanc fabricam apparo ,
sections.translation: Now I adopt this plan , and prepare this contrivance ,

sections.label: Section 5.2.1100
sections.original: ut te allegemus , filias dicas tuas
sections.translation: that we should cite you ; you are to affirm that they are your daughters , and that they were stolen when little from Carthage , and to maintain the cause of both in an action on their freedom , as though they were both your own daughters . Do you understand ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1104
sections.original: Intellego hercle . nam mihi item gnatae duae
sections.translation: On my faith , I do understand ; for I likewise did have two daughters

sections.label: Section 5.2.1105
sections.original: cum nutrice una sunt surruptae parvolae .
sections.translation: who were stolen away when little children , together with their nurse .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1106
sections.original: Lepide hercle adsimulas . iam in principio id mihi placet .
sections.translation: Upon my word , you do feign it cleverly . At the very commencement this amuses me .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1107
sections.translation: Much more , i ’ troth , than I could wish .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1107b
sections.translation: Dear me ! a subtle person , upon my word , artful and knowing , both tricky and crafty ! How he does whimper , in order that with his gestures he may effect this all the more easily .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1110
sections.original: me quoque dolis iam superat architectonem .
sections.translation: Even myself , now , the master-workman , does he excel in skill .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1111
sections.original: Sed earum nutrix qua sit facie , mi expedi .
sections.translation: But their nurse [*] , of what appearance was she ? Tell me .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1112
sections.translation: Of stature not tall , of a dusky complexion [*] .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1112b
sections.translation: ’ Tis the very person .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1113
sections.original: Specie venusta , óre atque oculis pernigris .
sections.translation: Of agreable form , with a small mouth , and very dark eyes .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1114
sections.original: Formam quidem hercle verbis depinxti probe .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , you really have depicted her form exactly in your words .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1115
sections.translation: Should you like to see her ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1115b
sections.translation: I ’ d rather see my daughters . Still , go and call her out of doors . If they are my daughters , if she is their nurse , she ’ ll recognize me at once .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1118
sections.original: Heus , ecquis hic est ? nuntiate ut prodeat
sections.translation: Hallo there ! is there any one here ? Tell Giddeneme to come out of doors ; there ’ s a person wants to see her .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1120
sections.translation: Who is it that knocks ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.1120b
sections.translation: One that ’ s a near acquaintance of yours .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1120c
sections.translation: What do you want ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.1120d
sections.translation: Come now ( pointing to HANNO .) do you know that person in the tunic , who it is ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.1122
sections.original: Nam quém ego aspicio ? pro supreme Iuppiter ,
sections.translation: Why , whom do I behold ? O supreme Jupiter ! this sure is my master , the father of my foster-children , Hanno , the Carthaginian !

sections.label: Section 5.3.1124b
sections.translation: Now , do see the cunning hussey this Carthaginian is really a clever juggler ; he has brought all over to his own opinion .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1127
sections.original: O mí ere , salve , Hánno , insperatissume
sections.translation: O my master ! welcome to you , Hanno ! most unhoped for by myself and your daughters , welcome to you ! But look you , don ’ t be wondering , or gazing so intently upon me .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1130
sections.translation: Don ’ t you know Giddeneme , your female slave ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.1131
sections.original: Novi . sed ubi sunt meae gnatae ? id scire expeto .
sections.translation: I know her . But where are my daughters ? That I ’ m longing to know .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1132
sections.translation: At the Temple of Venus [*] .
